question:
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
Six persons - P, Q, R, S, T, and U are sitting on a
triangular table such that all of them are facing
towards the center, but not necessarily in the
same order. Three persons are sitting on the
corners while three persons are sitting in the
middle of the sides of the table. Q sits third to
the right of R. Only one person sits between Q
and T, who is immediate left of R. As many
persons sit between S and T is the same as
between Q and S,
who is an immediate
neighbour of R. P sits second to the left of U. P
does not sit on the corner of the table.
Which of the following statement(s) is/are true
with respect to the final arrangement?

options.text: Three persons sits between T and P
P sits to the immediate left of S
P sits third to the left of Q
No one sits between U and Q

explanation:
Final answer:
Q sits third to the right of R. Only one person sits
between Q and T, who is immediate left of R.
From these conditions, we get,
As many persons sit between S and T is the
same as between Q and S,
who is an
immediate neighbour of R. P sits second to the
left of U.
P does not sit on the corner of the table. From
this condition Case, 1 gets rejected, and we get
Hence, No one sits between U and Q is true.

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
There are six people i.e. M, N, O, P, Q and R
sitting around a triangular-shaped table. Two
people sit in the middle of each of the three
sides. All are facing outside the triangular table.
All the information is not necessarily in the same
order.
N sits fourth to the right of Q. P sits fourth to the
left of R. There are three people sitting between
P and M. There are two people sitting between
M and O.
How many people sit between N and Q ?
explanation:
P sits fourth to the left of R.
There are three people sitting between P and
M.
There are two people sitting between M and O.
N sits fourth to the right of Q.

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
There are six people i.e. J, K, L, M, N and O
sitting around a triangular-shaped table. Two
people sit in the middle of each of the three
sides. Some of them are facing inside and some
of them are facing outside the table. All the
information is not necessarily in the same order.
N sits second to the left of M. There is only one
person sitting between N and K, who faces
outside the table. L sits third to the left of K and
both are facing opposite directions to each
other. J sits second to the left of L, and does
not sit near to M. The immediate neighbors of N
face the opposite direction to each other. O sits
second to the right of J. O and N facing the
same direction. N faces the opposite direction
to K.
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way and so form a group. Which one does not
belong to that group?
explanation: N sits second to the left of M.

is not opposite to Q.

not opposite to F.

10)O is not immediate neighbour of M and P.
11)Q is not opposite to H. P is not opposite to G.
12)R is not sitting immediate right of Q.
this is the final arrangement
